outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: US intelligence shows Israel preparing to strike Iranian nuclear sites if Trump-Iran talks collapse
outlets.summary: CNN reported that US intelligence indicates Israel is preparing plans to strike Iranian nuclear facilities — including Natanz, Fordow, and Arak — if diplomacy fails. Intercepted Israeli communications and munitions movements observed; US officials say no final decision has been made.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Israeli airstrikes kill 19 in southern Lebanon, including children, despite ceasefire
outlets.summary: Washington Post reported Israeli strikes killed 19 people in southern Lebanon including 3 children and 4 women across three towns — Deir Qanun al-Nahr (10 killed), Nabatieh (4 killed), and Kfar Sir (5 killed) — despite the 45-day ceasefire extension.

outlets.name: CBS News
outlets.headline: Gulf states talked Trump out of Iran strikes in a historic diplomatic intervention
outlets.summary: CBS News covered how Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE lobbied Trump to postpone a scheduled Iran strike, fearing Iranian retaliation against Gulf territory. Described it as an unprecedented show of Gulf diplomatic leverage over US military decisions.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: CENTCOM chief unloads after Democrat asks how many more Americans must die in Iran war
outlets.summary: Fox News covered CENTCOM commander Admiral Brad Cooper sharply rebuking Democratic Rep. Seth Moulton at a House Armed Services Committee hearing — calling his question about American deaths in Iran "inappropriate." Democrats pressed on war powers, costs, and strategy.

outlets.name: Washington Times
outlets.headline: Vance urges Iran to make a deal instead of facing "option B" — locked and loaded
outlets.summary: Washington Times covered VP Vance's blunt ultimatum to Iran: accept a deal preventing nuclear weapons or face renewed military bombardment — "Option B." Vance said the US is "locked and loaded" and that the conflict "is not going to be the sort of thing that lasts forever."

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.headline: NYT: Israeli war plans included installing Ahmadinejad as Iran leader after Khamenei assassination
outlets.summary: The Hill covered the explosive New York Times report revealing that US-Israeli war planning had included a scenario to install former Iranian President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as a post-war transitional leader following the assassination of Supreme Leader Khamenei.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war Day 82: Trump cancels strike; Israel readies nuclear plans; Xi-Putin call for ceasefire
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera's May 20 liveblog covers Trump's formal strike cancellation, CNN's report on Israeli nuclear facility strike preparations, the Xi-Putin summit calling for comprehensive ceasefire, and Araghchi's "many more surprises" warning.

outlets.name: Euronews
outlets.headline: Gulf states talk Trump out of Iran strikes in a show of regional influence
outlets.summary: Euronews analyzed how Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE successfully lobbied Trump to postpone a planned Iran strike, framing the episode as a significant demonstration of Gulf diplomatic leverage over Washington and a sign of how the conflict has empowered regional intermediaries.

outlets.name: Gulf News
outlets.headline: Putin meets Xi as Trump delays Iran strike and Gulf tensions deepen
outlets.summary: Gulf News covered the simultaneous Xi-Putin Beijing summit and Trump's Iran strike postponement, noting the diplomatic backdrop: both Russia and China are coordinating on Iran diplomacy while the US military remains at "a moment's notice" readiness.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: NYT: Israeli war plans sought to have Ahmadinejad run Iran after Khamenei killed
outlets.summary: Times of Israel's May 20 liveblog led with the explosive NYT report that US-Israeli war planning included a post-Khamenei scenario with Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as Iran's transitional leader. Separately: IDF Major Itamar Sapir killed in southern Lebanon — 10th IDF death since Lebanon ceasefire.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: IDF officer killed in Lebanon as Israel strikes kill 19 civilians; Lebanon death toll now 3,061
outlets.summary: Haaretz reported Major Itamar Sapir killed by Hezbollah fire in Qouzah — the 10th IDF death since the Lebanon ceasefire — as Israeli strikes killed 19 in southern Lebanon including children. Lebanon's cumulative death toll now stands at 3,061.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Major Itamar Sapir killed by Hezbollah fire in southern Lebanon — 10th IDF death since ceasefire
outlets.summary: Jerusalem Post reported Major (res.) Itamar Sapir, 27, was killed in Qouzah when Hezbollah fired on IDF troops from inside a church. He leaves behind a wife and 1.5-year-old son. Hezbollah simultaneously sent a drone into Israeli airspace and launched rockets at IDF positions.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Araghchi: Return to war will feature "many more surprises" — Iran prepared and stronger than before
outlets.summary: Iranian state outlet Press TV amplified FM Araghchi's warning that if war resumes, Iran has "many more surprises" in store — signaling readiness to escalate far beyond Phase 1. Iran is framing the 2–3 day diplomatic window as a US ultimatum, not a mutual negotiating pause.

outlets.name: Tasnim News
outlets.headline: Iran rejects NYT "Ahmadinejad plan" as fabrication; calls it evidence of US-Israel regime change agenda
outlets.summary: Iranian state-linked Tasnim News dismissed the New York Times' report on Israeli war plans to install Ahmadinejad as post-war Iranian leader, calling it a fabrication and using it to argue that the US-Israel alliance seeks illegal regime change in Iran — not a nuclear deal.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Majority of Israelis oppose ending Iran war under current conditions, poll shows
outlets.summary: Iran International, the London-based Persian-language outlet, reported a poll finding that a majority of Israelis believe ending the Iran war under current conditions contradicts Israeli security interests — reflecting hard-line Israeli public opinion against a ceasefire that leaves Iran's nuclear program intact.
